mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding output capacity and stronger corporate earnings, which directly supports higher equity valuations over the near term.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative median forward return in months with INDPRO > 0.5% monthly gain.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 4.0%, the tightening monetary policy increases discount rates and reduces equity risk appetite, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, the median 60-day SPX return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), or the signal is active less than 5% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate combined with elevated housing starts indicates an overheating economy that prompts preemptive policy tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition holds, the mean 20-day SPX return is positive, or the proportion of negative returns is not statistically above 60% (binomial test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 3% year-over-year reduces real interest rates when the Fed is slow to react, which drives investors toward equities as an inflation hedge, generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY > 3.0%, the median 20-day SPX return is negative, or the strategy fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline over any two-year period (Sharpe ratio lower).

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth signals robust consumer spending, which directly feeds into higher corporate revenues and profits, leading to immediate positive equity market reactions within the week.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between monthly RSAFS growth and subsequent 5-day SPX returns is non-positive over any 3-year window, or the mean return in top quartile of RSAFS growth is not significantly greater than bottom quartile (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals a deteriorating labor market that erodes consumer confidence and spending, leading to lower corporate earnings and negative equity returns over the next several months.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 5.5%, the median 60-day SPX return is not negative, or the frequency of positive returns exceeds 50% in triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls indicate a broadly expanding economy with rising output and employment, which jointly drive higher equity valuations through both earnings growth and investor confidence.
failure_condition: The joint signal of both INDPRO and PAYEMS rising month-over-month fails to produce a positive mean 20-day SPX return, or the interaction effect is not significantly additive beyond individual series (Wald test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts decline while the Fed is actively raising rates, the dual signals of weakening housing demand and monetary tightening foreshadow an economic slowdown, causing negative forward equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST decreasing for 3 consecutive months and FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
failure_condition: When the trigger condition activates, the median 60-day SPX return is non-negative, or the signal occurs fewer than 10 times in the walk-forward period.
